title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The development of linear perspective was a defining moment in Western art, (0) which allowed painters to create the illusion of depth on a flat surface. (9) Although artists had previously attempted to represent space, their techniques were often inconsistent and lacked mathematical precision. (10) Such was the impact of Filippo Brunelleschi’s discovery that it revolutionized how the world was depicted in the fifteenth century. (11) Provided that an artist followed the rules of the vanishing point, they could achieve a realistic sense of three-dimensional space. (12) Not only did this technique change painting, but it also influenced architecture and urban planning during the Renaissance. (13) Regardless of the subject matter, the use of perspective gave works a newfound authority and intellectual depth. (14) Not until the early 1900s did modern movements like Cubism begin to deliberately reject these traditional rules of representation. (15) Despite the passage of centuries, the principles of perspective remain fundamental to classical training in many art academies today. (16) Whether perspective is seen as a scientific tool or an artistic choice is a question that continues to occupy art historians. This mathematical approach to creativity forever altered the course of visual history and our perception of reality.

example.text: The development of linear perspective was a defining moment in Western art, (0) which allowed painters to create the illusion of depth.
example.explanation: Relative pronoun used to add non-essential information about the 'moment'.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did modern movements... begin').
Preposition used to introduce a contrast.
Conjunction introducing two possibilities or a doubt.
